session.title: Jenn's Ventures AI Night
session.subtitle: Master aligned session model
session.notes: Pre-populated from the live-site alignment pass. Restores the missing concept-first AI and ChatGPT teaching layer, then carries the participant workbook, pathways, image activity, reflection, and safe-use close.

steps.title: Arrival, reassurance, and opening question
steps.goal: Reduce pressure, set tone, connect the night to a real learner need.
steps.learner_prompt: Write one thing you hope AI could help you do better by the end of tonight.
steps.reflection_question: Why did you choose that need?
steps.facilitator_notes: Keep this calm. Make it clear that the night is about guided learning, not being perfect.

steps.title: Foundations: what AI is, what ChatGPT is, and what it is not
steps.goal: Introduce AI, ChatGPT, usefulness, limits, and why output still needs checking.
steps.learner_prompt: Listen for one thing AI can do well and one thing it can get wrong.
steps.reflection_question: What surprised you about what AI is or is not?
steps.facilitator_notes: Cover AI, ChatGPT, prompt, hallucination, bias, drift, and verification in plain language.

steps.title: Weak prompt versus stronger prompt
steps.goal: Show that prompt quality changes usefulness.
steps.improved_prompt: Plan a low-cost Saturday for a teen in Kitchener-Waterloo with one outdoor idea, one indoor backup, and a timeline from noon to 6 PM.
steps.reflection_question: What changed because the prompt became more specific?
steps.facilitator_notes: Teach audience, format, and limits here.

steps.title: Workbook start and setup
steps.goal: Move learners into the workbook after the concept intro and first demo.
steps.learner_prompt: Choose your topic, your level, and what you expect the tool to help you do.

steps.title: Guided text prompt practice
steps.goal: Give every learner one successful prompt-improvement loop early in the session.

activity_cards.weak_prompt: Plan something fun for this weekend.
activity_cards.better_prompt_hint: Add audience, place, budget, time, and output format.
Add topic, age, format, and what would make the answer useful.
Add audience, tone, length, and what needs to be clear.

steps.learner_prompt: Improve one weak prompt. Save the better version. Note what changed in the output.
steps.reflection_question: What did you expect the improved prompt to return, and what did it actually return?

steps.title: Break and regroup
steps.facilitator_notes: Protect the break. Do not let the room run hot for the full 90 minutes.

steps.title: Image prompt demo
steps.goal: Show that image prompts improve with audience, purpose, layout, and style.
steps.improved_prompt: Create a friendly poster for a youth AI night at camp. Use a clean layout, bold heading, warm evening colours, and space for date, time, and what to bring.
steps.reflection_question: What details helped most in the better image prompt?

steps.title: Guided image activity
steps.goal: Give learners one supported image-generation task without overload.
steps.learner_prompt: Create one image prompt with purpose, audience, style, and layout clearly stated.

steps.title: Advanced or future-path demo
steps.goal: Show what comes after browser-first use without making it the core of the night.
steps.facilitator_notes: Keep this short. Position it as optional future direction.

steps.title: Choose Guided, Build, Remix, or Stretch
steps.goal: Right-size the final work block to different learner confidence levels.

branch_rules.next_focus: compare multiple outputs and name verification needs

steps.title: Personal mini-project
steps.goal: Use the prompt-improvement pattern on a personally relevant task.
steps.learner_prompt: Create one prompt for a real interest: school, creativity, hobbies, planning, or a useful life task. Save the best version and note one improvement you made.

steps.title: Reflection and summary
steps.goal: Capture learning, remaining gaps, and next-step thinking.
steps.learner_prompt: What changed? What still needs checking? What would you do differently next time?
steps.facilitator_notes: Do not skip the verification habit.

steps.title: Safe-use close and takeaway
steps.goal: End with one useful habit and one realistic next step.
steps.learner_prompt: Write one prompt habit you want to keep using after tonight.
